Alright, JB here. Time for your daily motivation slash skills training. It's Friday. You better be done with module three. If you're not, come on. You got to catch up here. I assume you're done though, right? I hope you've been through all your quizzes, your exercises. You should have set your anchor if you haven't. You got to do it now. Alright? So I'm going to talk past that because I spoke about that already the rest of this week. Let me just give you some food authority, alright? I speak a lot about state management for the purposes of business because it's a business training. We want to make more money. We want to close more sales. Being asked to the company that we work for, being asked to our own company, help other people come to the company. That's what we're doing here, right? But just remember this. State management is for all aspects of life. There are states that you want to be in for your family and your children that are very different in certainty and clarity and confidence and courage. How about a state of compassion? A state of love, understanding, empathy. Those states matter too. Just remember that. I don't ever want you to take this module and have it shift how you move through the world entirely, meaning you don't want to manage your state at this ultra-high level every single moment of every day. It's idiotic as possible and it'll make you into an A-hole. Please use this stuff wisely. I teach stuff that is powerful and that works. You know that, right? All I'm saying is that you can also use the same technique for a state of patience. Next time you're feeling really calm and zen-like and ultimate patience, do a different anchor. Use a different sense of smell. I'll tell you a funny story. I was on a plane, I think I was on Emirates going from LA to Dubai. First class, thank God, right? In the first class, they have a private cabin. They had a little console, your beauty console, they have little things, creams and this. You know what they had? They had four different scents there. They had a scent for sleep, a scent for wakefulness, a scent for a look. They actually were using scents of smell to help you trigger certain states. That won't work because unless you're setting it at the top of your anchor, it won't matter. But the point was they'd identified that there were certain smells that naturally align with certain states. This is powerful. Point, you can use this for anything. You want to be able to fall asleep then and next time you're really, really tired. You have that uncontrollable, sleepy feeling. Take a snort of something that is extreme but pleasing to you that actually would make you sleepy and link that to the sleepy feeling. And you'll find yourself setting an anchor for sleep. Point is you can set that for anything. Anchoring is a process that is not just relegated for certainty. That's my gift to you. I love you all. Great week. Now we're going back to the outer world this week. Coming up is tonality, advanced tonality. I'm going to take on what we learned in unconscious communication and you are going to die. This next module is so awesome. I worked on it for like two and a half, three months on one module. The graphics department went crazy with this module. It was really complex to make sure that you were able to leverage off of the graphics. You're going to love it. And I will see you for short, fun motivations on science Sunday this week for those of you who want certification. The rest of you, I'll see you on Monday. Take care.
